initial_layer_bed_temp.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj + initial_layer_bed_offset + (PETG?+5:0), clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

initial_layer_temp.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + initial_layer_nozzle_offset, optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

other_layers_bed_temp.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

other_layers_temp.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset, optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

prov.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: ABS bonds too aggressively or too weakly to bare plate вЂ” glue is required for reliable adhesion.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (required)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (80В°C / 5h)

checklist.detail: ABS emits VOCs during printing. Open windows or use an air purifier. Do not print in unventilated spaces.
checklist.text: Ensure good room ventilation

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: Essential for ABS/ASA вЂ” these materials warp severely at corners without a brim.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Fan off вЂ” this material needs slow, even cooling to prevent warping and layer cracking.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Moderate inner wall speed helps ABS/ASA cool uniformly between layers.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on A1 prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Slower outer walls reduce warping risk by allowing each layer more time to cool gradually.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Standard retraction distance for this material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

    Bottom shell layers: 4

    Bottom surface pattern: Monotonic
    Internal solid infill pattern: Rectilinear
    Sparse infill density: 15%
    Sparse infill pattern: Grid

  Speed

    Inner wall speed: 100 mm/s

  Others

warnings.text: ABS emits styrene VOCs during printing. Use enclosed printer with carbon filter. Ensure good room ventilation вЂ” do not print in unventilated spaces.

warnings.detail: ABS requires a fully enclosed printer. High warping risk вЂ” consider PLA or PETG instead.
warnings.text: A1 is an open-frame printer.

warnings.detail: ABS typically prints best at 100В°C nominal (105В°C on the initial layer). Your printer's bed maxes at 100В°C, so the emitted profile clamps the bed target. Prints may warp or fail layer adhesion вЂ” consider switching to a material with a lower bed requirement.
warnings.text: A1 bed temperature capped at 100В°C.

warnings.detail: This material requires a stable enclosed environment to prevent warping and layer cracking. Use a draft shield, close nearby doors/windows, add a wide brim (8вЂ“12mm), and consider a DIY enclosure.
warnings.text: ABS has high shrinkage risk on an open printer.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

bridge_flow.why: Slight under-extrusion on bridges reduces sagging вЂ” PETG is particularly prone to bridge droop at full flow.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Moderate fan вЂ” balances cooling for dimensional accuracy without causing adhesion issues.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on A1 prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: A1 tops out at 10,000 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: PETG Basic can bond too aggressively to smooth PEI вЂ” glue acts as a release agent.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (recommended)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (65В°C / 8h)

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

bridge_flow.why: Slight under-extrusion on bridges reduces sagging вЂ” PETG is particularly prone to bridge droop at full flow.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Moderate fan вЂ” balances cooling for dimensional accuracy without causing adhesion issues.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on A1 Mini prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: A1 Mini tops out at 10,000 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Standard retraction distance for this material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

warnings.detail: PETG prints better with stable ambient temperature. Consider a DIY enclosure or warm room to avoid layer separation.

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: ABS bonds too aggressively or too weakly to bare plate вЂ” glue is required for reliable adhesion.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (required)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (80В°C / 5h)

checklist.detail: ABS emits VOCs during printing. Open windows or use an air purifier. Do not print in unventilated spaces.
checklist.text: Ensure good room ventilation

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: Essential for ABS/ASA вЂ” these materials warp severely at corners without a brim.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Fan off вЂ” this material needs slow, even cooling to prevent warping and layer cracking.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Moderate inner wall speed helps ABS/ASA cool uniformly between layers.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on Aries prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Slower outer walls reduce warping risk by allowing each layer more time to cool gradually.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Bowden retraction increased to compensate for the longer PTFE tube path. Scaled for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune based on tube length.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: ABS bonds too aggressively or too weakly to bare plate вЂ” glue is required for reliable adhesion.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (required)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (80В°C / 5h)

checklist.detail: ABS requires a warm chamber. Set bed to target temp and wait before starting the print.
checklist.text: Preheat enclosure for 15 minutes before starting

checklist.detail: ABS emits VOCs during printing. Open windows or use an air purifier. Do not print in unventilated spaces.
checklist.text: Ensure good room ventilation

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: Essential for ABS/ASA вЂ” these materials warp severely at corners without a brim.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Fan off вЂ” this material needs slow, even cooling to prevent warping and layer cracking.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration helps ABS/ASA maintain consistent layer bonding.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Moderate inner wall speed helps ABS/ASA cool uniformly between layers.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration helps ABS/ASA cool more uniformly, reducing warping.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Slower outer walls reduce warping risk by allowing each layer more time to cool gradually.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Standard retraction distance for this material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) bonds too aggressively or too weakly to bare plate вЂ” glue is required for reliable adhesion.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (required)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) is highly moisture-sensitive вЂ” wet filament causes bubbles, stringing, and weak layers.
checklist.text: Dry filament before printing (80В°C / 5h)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) requires a warm chamber. Set bed to target temp and wait before starting the print.
checklist.text: Preheat enclosure for 15 minutes before starting

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) emits VOCs during printing. Open windows or use an air purifier. Do not print in unventilated spaces.
checklist.text: Ensure good room ventilation

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: PC shrinks significantly вЂ” a wide brim is essential to prevent corner lift and delamination.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Fan off вЂ” this material needs slow, even cooling to prevent warping and layer cracking.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Higher than outer wall вЂ” inner walls are hidden so acceleration artifacts are not visible.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Inner walls are hidden вЂ” higher speed than outer walls trades invisible quality for faster prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Outer wall acceleration tuned for your printer вЂ” balances speed with surface quality.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Outer wall speed balanced for your printer type вЂ” CoreXY handles higher speeds without ringing.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Longer retraction to prevent ooze with this high-temp material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Gyroid provides uniform strength in all three axes. The nozzle never crosses existing infill lines in the same layer, reducing pressure spikes.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Walls contribute more to strength than infill. Going from 2 to 4 walls often doubles part strength at lower cost than raising infill.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

  3D Print Assistant вЂ” Profile Export
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

Printer:          K1 Max
Nozzle:           Standard 0.4 mm
Material:         PC (Polycarbonate)
Slicer:           OrcaSlicer
Surface quality:  Standard
Strength:         Strong
Speed priority:   Balanced
Environment:      Normal room

в”Ђв”Ђ Filament Settings в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  (Filament tab in OrcaSlicer)

  TEMPERATURE
    Nozzle temp (initial):   285 В°C
    Nozzle temp (other):     280 В°C
    Bed temp:                100 В°C  (same for both layers)

  FAN SETTINGS
    Part cooling fan:        0вЂ“20%
    Fan speed (min):         0%
    Overhang fan speed:      20
    Min layer time:          15 s

  SPEED LIMIT
    Max volumetric speed:    10 mmВі/s

  SETUP
    Build plate:             Engineering Plate (glue required)
    AMS compatible:          Yes
    Drying:                  Critical вЂ” 80 В°C / 4вЂ“6h
    Enclosure:               Fully enclosed вЂ” required

  EXTRUSION
    Pressure advance:        0.045
    Flow ratio:              0.98
    Retraction length:       1 mm
    Retraction speed:        30 mm/s

в”Ђв”Ђ Print Profile Settings в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  (Process tab in OrcaSlicer)

  Quality
    Layer height: 0.2 mm
    First layer height: 0.20 mm
    Outer wall line width: 0.42 mm
    Inner wall line width: 0.44 mm
    Top surface line width: 0.42 mm
    Arc fitting: Enabled
    X-Y hole compensation: 0.05вЂ“0.1 mm
    Elephant foot compensation: 0.15 mm
    Wall generator: Classic
    Walls printing order: Inner / Outer
    Avoid crossing walls: Enabled

  Strength
    Wall loops: 4
    Top shell layers: 5
    Bottom shell layers: 5
    Top surface pattern: Monotonic
    Bottom surface pattern: Monotonic
    Internal solid infill pattern: Rectilinear
    Sparse infill density: 35%
    Sparse infill pattern: Gyroid

  Speed
    First layer: 30 mm/s
    Outer wall: 40 mm/s
    Inner wall: 60 mm/s
    Top surface: 60 mm/s
    Gap infill: 100 mm/s
    Outer wall: 5000 mm/sВІ
    Inner wall: 8000 mm/sВІ
    First layer: 500 mm/sВІ

  Others
    Brim width: 8вЂ“12 mm

в”Ђв”Ђ Warnings в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  вљ  Bed temperature 110В°C+: ensure your printer's bed can reach and sustain this temperature.
  вљ  K1 Max bed temperature capped at 100В°C.
  вљ  PC (Polycarbonate) bonds aggressively to smooth PEI.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Generated by 3dprintassistant.com

warnings.text: Bed temperature 110В°C+: ensure your printer's bed can reach and sustain this temperature.

warnings.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) typically prints best at 110В°C nominal. Your printer's bed maxes at 100В°C, so the emitted profile clamps the bed target. Prints may warp or fail layer adhesion вЂ” consider switching to a material with a lower bed requirement.
warnings.text: K1 Max bed temperature capped at 100В°C.

warnings.detail: Use a glue stick or hairspray as a release layer to prevent damage to the build plate surface. A textured PEI sheet is also a safe alternative.
warnings.text: PC (Polycarbonate) bonds aggressively to smooth PEI.

initial_layer_bed_temp.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj + initial_layer_bed_offset + (PETG?+5:0), clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

initial_layer_temp.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + initial_layer_nozzle_offset, optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

other_layers_bed_temp.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

other_layers_temp.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset, optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

prov.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) bonds too aggressively or too weakly to bare plate вЂ” glue is required for reliable adhesion.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (required)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) is highly moisture-sensitive вЂ” wet filament causes bubbles, stringing, and weak layers.
checklist.text: Dry filament before printing (80В°C / 5h)

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) requires a warm chamber. Set bed to target temp and wait before starting the print.
checklist.text: Preheat enclosure for 15 minutes before starting

checklist.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) emits VOCs during printing. Open windows or use an air purifier. Do not print in unventilated spaces.
checklist.text: Ensure good room ventilation

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: PC shrinks significantly вЂ” a wide brim is essential to prevent corner lift and delamination.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Fan off вЂ” this material needs slow, even cooling to prevent warping and layer cracking.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Higher than outer wall вЂ” inner walls are hidden so acceleration artifacts are not visible.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Inner walls are hidden вЂ” higher speed than outer walls trades invisible quality for faster prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Outer wall acceleration tuned for your printer вЂ” balances speed with surface quality.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Outer wall speed balanced for your printer type вЂ” CoreXY handles higher speeds without ringing.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Longer retraction to prevent ooze with this high-temp material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Gyroid provides uniform strength in all three axes. The nozzle never crosses existing infill lines in the same layer, reducing pressure spikes.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Walls contribute more to strength than infill. Going from 2 to 4 walls often doubles part strength at lower cost than raising infill.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

warnings.text: Bed temperature 110В°C+: ensure your printer's bed can reach and sustain this temperature.

warnings.detail: PC (Polycarbonate) typically prints best at 110В°C nominal. Your printer's bed maxes at 100В°C, so the emitted profile clamps the bed target. Prints may warp or fail layer adhesion вЂ” consider switching to a material with a lower bed requirement.
warnings.text: K1 Max bed temperature capped at 100В°C.

warnings.detail: Use a glue stick or hairspray as a release layer to prevent damage to the build plate surface. A textured PEI sheet is also a safe alternative.
warnings.text: PC (Polycarbonate) bonds aggressively to smooth PEI.

initial_layer_bed_temp.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj + initial_layer_bed_offset + (PETG?+5:0), clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

initial_layer_temp.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + initial_layer_nozzle_offset, optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

bed_temperature.why: Bed temperature for layers after the first.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: PETG Basic can bond too aggressively to smooth PEI вЂ” glue acts as a release agent.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (recommended)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (65В°C / 8h)

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

bridge_flow.why: Slight under-extrusion on bridges reduces sagging вЂ” PETG is particularly prone to bridge droop at full flow.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Moderate fan вЂ” balances cooling for dimensional accuracy without causing adhesion issues.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on Mega X prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Mega X tops out at 400 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Bowden retraction increased to compensate for the longer PTFE tube path. Scaled for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune based on tube length.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

Nozzle:           Standard 0.4 mm

Slicer:           OrcaSlicer
Surface quality:  Standard

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

bridge_flow.why: Slight under-extrusion on bridges reduces sagging вЂ” PETG is particularly prone to bridge droop at full flow.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Moderate fan вЂ” balances cooling for dimensional accuracy without causing adhesion issues.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on Mega X prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Mega X tops out at 400 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Bowden retraction increased to compensate for the longer PTFE tube path. Scaled for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune based on tube length.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Full fan speed вЂ” rapid cooling locks in detail and prevents sagging on overhangs.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

ironing.why: Auto-enabled at Fine or better surface quality. Ironing re-passes the nozzle over the top surface to produce a near-smooth finish.

layer_height.why: Fine layers visibly reduce the staircase effect on curved and angled surfaces.

only_one_wall_top.why: Using a single outer wall on top layers reduces the gaps between adjacent wall lines, producing a smoother and more uniform top surface.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on MK4 prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: MK4 tops out at 8,000 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Standard retraction distance for this material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

seam_position.why: At fine quality, the seam is more visible. Placing it consistently at one location makes it easy to hide or orient away from view.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Slow top surface speed produces the most uniform and flat top layer вЂ” critical when ironing is enabled.

wall_generator.why: Arachne uses variable-width extrusion to fill thin walls and fine details that Classic would leave partially empty.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: PETG HF can bond too aggressively to smooth PEI вЂ” glue acts as a release agent.
checklist.text: Apply glue stick to build plate (recommended)

checklist.detail: PETG HF is highly moisture-sensitive вЂ” wet filament causes bubbles, stringing, and weak layers.
checklist.text: Dry filament before printing (65В°C / 8h)

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

bridge_flow.why: Slight under-extrusion on bridges reduces sagging вЂ” PETG is particularly prone to bridge droop at full flow.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Moderate fan вЂ” balances cooling for dimensional accuracy without causing adhesion issues.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

infill_combination.why: Combines infill from multiple layers into one pass. Significantly reduces print time with minimal strength impact on non-structural parts.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Reduced acceleration for bedslinger вЂ” inner walls still contribute to visible ringing.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Bedslinger printers benefit from lower inner wall speeds to reduce ringing on taller prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Lower acceleration on MK4S prevents ringing from the moving print bed mass.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: MK4S tops out at 8,000 mm/sВІ acceleration вЂ” higher outer-wall speeds cause ringing on tall prints.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.4mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Standard retraction distance for this material.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Grid is efficient and strong in the XY plane. A solid default for most everyday parts.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Standard wall count вЂ” good balance of strength, weight, and print time.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (55В°C / 6h)

checklist.detail: PLA-CF on an enclosed printer: keep door open to prevent heat creep when bed is above 45В°C.
checklist.text: Open front door + remove top glass panel

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Full fan speed вЂ” rapid cooling locks in detail and prevents sagging on overhangs.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Higher than outer wall вЂ” inner walls are hidden so acceleration artifacts are not visible.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Inner walls are hidden вЂ” higher speed than outer walls trades invisible quality for faster prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Outer wall acceleration tuned for your printer вЂ” balances speed with surface quality.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Outer wall speed balanced for your printer type вЂ” CoreXY handles higher speeds without ringing.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.6mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Retraction scaled for 0.6mm nozzle (base 0.6mm for 0.4mm) вЂ” small nozzles need less, large nozzles need more.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Gyroid provides uniform strength in all three axes. The nozzle never crosses existing infill lines in the same layer, reducing pressure spikes.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Walls contribute more to strength than infill. Going from 2 to 4 walls often doubles part strength at lower cost than raising infill.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

checklist.detail: Fingerprints and grease are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.
checklist.text: Clean build plate with IPA (isopropyl alcohol)

checklist.detail: Filament absorbs moisture from air over time. Drying improves surface quality and layer bonding.
checklist.text: Dry filament if not recently opened (55В°C / 6h)

checklist.detail: PLA-CF on an enclosed printer: keep door open to prevent heat creep when bed is above 45В°C.
checklist.text: Open front door + remove top glass panel

checklist.detail: Bambu printers auto-level вЂ” run it manually from the touchscreen if the printer was moved or hasn't been leveled recently.
checklist.text: Run bed leveling if first print or after moving printer

checklist.detail: Different brands vary in actual diameter and flow properties. Calibration ensures accurate extrusion.
checklist.text: Run flow calibration when switching filament brand or type

arc_fitting.why: Converts thousands of short linear segments into smooth arc moves (G2/G3). Reduces vibration and improves surface quality on circular features.

avoid_crossing_walls.why: Routes travel moves around printed walls rather than through them. Reduces the risk of seam blobs and surface zits from ooze during travel.

bottom_shell_layers.why: Solid bottom shells form the base of the print. More shells improve the first-layer surface quality visible on the underside.

bottom_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic bottom surface pattern ensures complete coverage on the first visible layers.

brim_width.why: A brim prevents corners and thin features from lifting off the bed during printing.

elephant_foot_compensation.why: Prevents the over-squeezed first layer from widening the base and affecting dimensional accuracy of features close to the bed.

fan_speed.why: Full fan speed вЂ” rapid cooling locks in detail and prevents sagging on overhangs.

gap_fill_speed.why: Gap fill handles small spaces between walls. Moderate speed prevents pressure buildup and ooze in tight gaps.

initial_layer_acceleration.why: Very low acceleration ensures the nozzle moves smoothly at slow speed вЂ” prevents the first layer from being dragged or lifting at corners.

initial_layer_height.why: A slightly thicker initial layer improves bed adhesion. Scales with nozzle size вЂ” small nozzles use thinner first layers, large nozzles use thicker ones.

initial_layer_speed.why: Slow initial layer speed gives the filament more time to bond with the bed surface and build a solid foundation.

inner_wall_acceleration.why: Higher than outer wall вЂ” inner walls are hidden so acceleration artifacts are not visible.

inner_wall_line_width.why: Wider inner walls print faster while maintaining structural integrity.

inner_wall_speed.why: Inner walls are hidden вЂ” higher speed than outer walls trades invisible quality for faster prints.

internal_solid_infill_pattern.why: Rectilinear is the fastest pattern for solid infill вЂ” the slicer alternates line direction per layer automatically.

layer_height.why: Standard balance of print time and surface quality for everyday parts.

order_of_walls.why: Inner walls are placed first, establishing the reference geometry. The outer wall is then placed precisely against them вЂ” improving hole and feature accuracy.

outer_wall_acceleration.why: Outer wall acceleration tuned for your printer вЂ” balances speed with surface quality.

outer_wall_line_width.why: Slightly wider than nozzle diameter вЂ” improves surface quality and wall strength with minimal speed penalty.

outer_wall_speed.why: Outer wall speed balanced for your printer type вЂ” CoreXY handles higher speeds without ringing.

pressure_advance.why: Pressure advance (K-factor) calibrated for 0.6mm nozzle. Fine-tune with a PA calibration test print.

retraction_distance.why: Retraction scaled for 0.6mm nozzle (base 0.6mm for 0.4mm) вЂ” small nozzles need less, large nozzles need more.

retraction_speed.why: Retraction speed tuned for this material's melt characteristics.

sparse_infill_density.why: Balanced infill вЂ” enough internal structure for everyday parts without excessive material use.

sparse_infill_pattern.why: Gyroid provides uniform strength in all three axes. The nozzle never crosses existing infill lines in the same layer, reducing pressure spikes.

top_shell_layers.why: More top shells prevent pillowing (small holes in the top face) вЂ” common at low infill densities or with fine layer heights.

top_surface_line_width.why: Matching the outer wall width gives a consistent, uniform top surface appearance.

top_surface_pattern.why: Monotonic line produces the smoothest top surface вЂ” each line always starts where the previous ended, eliminating gaps and improving gloss.

top_surface_speed.why: Top surface speed tuned for your printer вЂ” slower than inner walls since this layer is visible.

wall_generator.why: Classic produces consistent, predictable wall widths вЂ” better for structural parts.

wall_loops.why: Walls contribute more to strength than infill. Going from 2 to 4 walls often doubles part strength at lower cost than raising infill.

xy_hole_compensation.why: Compensates for material expansion that makes holes slightly smaller than designed. Start at 0.05 mm and adjust with a test print.

bed.ref: bed_temp_base + env.bed_adj, optionally clamped to min(material.bed_temp_max, printer.max_bed_temp)

nozzle.ref: nozzle_temp_base + env.nozzle_adj + nozzle.temp_offset + (speed==="fast"?5:0), optionally clamped to min(material.nozzle_temp_max, printer.max_nozzle_temp)
